ch14_start.text: PA, inhumanly steady: 'WARNING. PROTOCOL OMEGA INITIATED. SCUTTLING CHARGES DETONATING IN T-MINUS TEN MINUTES.' The ocean is not merely winning. Someone has signed the loss. Optional paths are over. What remains is evidence, speed, and the last clean room that still has a door.

ch14_logs.text: Not automated. Not a cascade trip from flood sensors. Manual initiation. Authorization biometrics match GIDEON STROUD — timestamped after Corporate's one-line acceptance, before most of the crew knew the word Omega. He is not reacting to the end. He is conducting it.

choices.text: Save the auth log. Track his tag.

ch14_track_gideon.text: Internal sensors place his biometric tag in the Escape Bay. One Bathysphere remains green on the board. Crew capacity: one. The math he has always preferred, reduced to a seat.

ch14_run.text: Corridors collapse into suggestions of corridors. Water needles through seams. Behind the roar of metal, wet footsteps of the assimilated keep pace like a second heart learning your route. You pass a wall screen still looping Gideon's calm face from an old safety brief: REMAIN ORDERLY. The irony nearly kills you before the depth can.

textVariants.text: You run and the station runs with you — in your veins, in the chorus that has learned your stride. Miller's voice offers a shortcut that would drown you. You take the long way on purpose.

choices.text: Push through to the Escape Bay.

ch14_end.text: A structural beam seals the corridor at your back with the finality of a period. Forward: Escape Bay blast doors, amber launch lights bleeding underneath. Behind: the rising dark. There is only one way left that still pretends to be a choice.
